steps.title: Identify the parallel signal
steps.content: The pineapple’s structure is “no longer identical to” its wild ancestor — it differs from it. The word “also” signals that summer squash has the same type of relationship to its wild ancestor. The following sentence confirms this: humans bred the squash from hard and bitter to soft and mild.

steps.title: Eliminate three choices, confirm one
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘varies from’: “Varies from” means is different from, which matches the pineapple parallel and the evidence that domestication changed the squash’s traits. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘helps with’: Implies the squash assists its wild ancestor. The passage describes difference, not assistance. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘argues with’: Suggests confrontation or disagreement, which does not apply to a biological relationship between a domesticated plant and its ancestor. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘reacts to’: Describes a response, not a state of difference. The passage establishes that the domesticated squash differs from the wild form, not that it reacts to it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
